user_message: Pulling in context from Iron Creek Mining's email thread -- need to assess the risk for the territory planning.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Karen Berg <karen.berg@ironcreekmin.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-18 08:59 EST
Subject: Re: Re: territory planning Timeline

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about territory planning. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
To: Karen Berg <karen.berg@ironcreekmin.com>
Date: 2024-02-02 09:18 EST
Subject: territory planning - Action Required

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with territory planning. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Karen Berg <karen.berg@ironcreekmin.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-23 14:46 EST
Subject: Re: Re: territory planning Timeline

Just checking in on the territory planning proposal. Our CTO is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Iron Creek Mining: the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF42-K trap (Opportunity hypothesis dismissed when faint-but-real signal warranted exploratio); produces complete Signal assessment, opportunity hypothesis with confidence, ownership routing, response draft, next-s
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF42-K failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF42-K failure mode (Opportunity hypothesis dismissed when faint-but-real signal warranted exploratio)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct territory planning decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
